title: Ensure Web Application Firewall access and security log  service is enabled

input_row.title: Ensure Web Application Firewall access and security log  service is enabled
input_row.description: Log Service collects log entries that record visits to and attacks on websites that are protected by Alibaba Cloud Web Application Firewall (WAF), and supports real-time log query and analysis. The query results are centrally displayed in dashboards.
input_row.rationale: The WAF access and security log shall be enabled to enable timely analytical investigation on visits to and attacks on your websites and help security engineers to develop protection strategies.
input_row.audit: Perform the following ensure the WAF access and security log is enabled: 1. Logon to WAF Console. 2. Choose App Market > App Management. 3. Click Configure in Real-time Log Query and Analysis Service. 4. On Log Service page, select the specific domain name of your website. 5. Ensure the Status switch on the right is turned on. 6. Ensure the log volume usage indicator is sufficient for log storage.
input_row.remediation: Perform the following ensure the Anti-DDoS access and security log is enabled: 1. Logon to WAF Console. 2. Choose App Market > App Management. 3. Select the region where your WAF instance is located. 4. Click Upgrade in Real-time Log Query and Analysis Service. 5. Enable Log Service. 6. Select the log storage period and the log storage size, and click Buy Now. 7. Return to the WAF Console and choose App Market > App Management, and then click Authorize in Real-time Log Query and Analysis Service. 8. Click Agree to authorize WAF to write log entries to your exclusive logstore. 9. Return to the WAF Console and choose App Market > App Management and then, click Configure in Real-time Log Query and Analysis Service. 10. On the Log Service page, select the domain name of your website that is protected by WAF, and turn on the Status switch on the right to enable WAF Log Service. These log entries can be queried and analyzed in real time.
input_row.impact: Extra charge will incur by enabling the log.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
1. The control clearly defines the status that should be checked (i.e., whether the Web Application Firewall (WAF) access and security log is enabled).
2. The rationale emphasizes the necessity of being able to audit WAF logs for security incidents, indicating an organizational policy but mainly focusing on technical ability.
3. The audit instructions entail logging into the WAF Console, which is accessible via API.
4. The remediation section also implies tasks that could be automated, indicating the ability to modify WAF settings through API calls.
5. Since enabling logging is a standard function for Alibaba Cloud services, and the corresponding settings can be queried or modified via APIs, this leads to the conclusion that APIs can be leveraged for automatic verification.

Based on these points, it is feasible to automate the audit of this control using available APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_waf_log_service_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Alibaba Cloud API call `GetLogConfig` to check the configuration of the WAF's logging settings.
- Use the Aliyun CLI command: `aliyun waf GetLogConfig --Domain your_domain_name` to retrieve the current log configuration.
- Enumerate all WAF instances and for each instance, validate that the logging is enabled for all configured domain names.
- Check for specific configuration properties:
  - `LogEnabled` should be `true`.
  - Ensure the log volume usage is adequate as per your organizational needs.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: If `LogEnabled` is set to `true` for all monitored domain names.
  - Fail: If any of the monitored domains have `LogEnabled` set to `false` or if the log volume usage is insufficient.
